actionable_predicate: Before the architecture review, each attendee should have answered for themselves: what is the technical truth of the proposal that I would have to read the appendix dashboards to verify? Attendees who arrive without that pre-loaded perception are awake to the room's political weather and asleep to the field the meeting is supposed to be deciding on.
example_decision: A team is preparing the quarterly architecture review. The PM applies this verse: each presenter must publish the appendix dashboards 48 hours before the review, and each attendee must read them before walking in. The decision predicate becomes: 'when you take your seat, is your perception loaded with the technical truth of the proposal, or with the room's politics?' The audit is the post-meeting reflection — record what each attendee said, count the ratio of technical-substrate comments to political-weather comments. If the ratio falls below threshold, the meeting was a status performance and the architecture decision was made elsewhere; the PM intervenes by re-running with mandatory pre-reading.
